reviews.review_msg: Amazing recorded lectures that gave you everything you needed to know, fair assignments and nicely distributed across the semester, pretty hard exams but you are given a cheat sheet and if you do the assignments you will do great. Very kind professor too.

DO NOT take stats 201 online with this professor. His recorded lectures were incredibly difficult to understand. On top of that the lectures slides posted never worked properly, most of the material was always cut out from the slides so I used ai/the textbook to teach myself the entire course.

Do not take it with him, not helpful at all and tells different students different grading criterias and makes no sense. He will make jokes and laugh at the fact of getting a C-/failing. He says theres going to be no r coding and there is on the final thats worth 55%. Theres about 3-5 lecture recording every week and material coursework is heavy.
Final exam worth 55% and was very hard.

Gamage's STAT 203 class is engaging and well-structured. He explains complex concepts clearly with practical examples, making statistics fun. Lectures are engaging and assignments reinforce learning effectively. His supportive style fosters success, though the final exam is heavy, so consistent studying is really essential.

Best stats prof by far, in his lectures he breaks down concepts into very simple concepts for students to understand. Sometimes he will even add in if itll be tested on the exam. Although the final was worth 50% it's an online course so I guess it's understandable. Just wish he provided more practice content but overall highly recommend
Chill professor that makes what might be the driest subject matter even somewhat palatable. Plenty of time was given for the assignments and the tests allowed for the use of TYPED cheat sheets.
Best statistics professor!!!

I took this course in spring. Harsha is a very chill and approachable professor. His explanations are clear and his expectations are reasonable. Manageable workload. Fair exams, though the final was a bit tricky. Practice assignment and textbook questions well to prepare for exams.
best prof at sfu , made concepts really easy to understand. He replies to emails super quick, even at 12am. The tests are fair and mainly come out of the textbook

The best professor I could have wanted for STAT270. His lectures are clear and assignments are straightforward. Exams are also fair. He is super approachable and is always looking to help students. He is constantly seeking feedback to improve his course which is something I respect. He is very caring and wants to see his students do well. 10/10

Dr. Perera genuinely cares about students. He goes slowly during lectures and repeats important concepts. His office hours are quite helpful and he often mentions what type of questions will be on the exam during class. Still, it is a hard class so make sure to consistently practice outside of class. Take STAT 270 with him if you're able to!
switched from scott pai's section to this one and im so glad. prof perera can actually teach and responds extremely quickly to emails

He's really kind and is able to teach a really dense subject efficiently, he also teaches slowly in chapter three (probabilities) which is really nice of him, but 270 is pretty hard in general so make sure to practice the textbook questions he assigns. Overall he's great, you can tell he knows what he's doing :)
Nice

STATS 270 was not an option but Harsha is the best prof I've had so far. He's very respectful and kind and is always wanting feedback from his students. His exams are very fair and realistic as well. Would recommend 10/10 and to go to his office hours even if you don't have any questions.

I had held back on taking STAT203 for years and finally took it with Harsha. He made it feel easier and took his time explaining concepts. The assignments really helped prepare for tests and he even allowed cheat sheets which really helped. Would definitely recommend taking his class, especially for statistics!

Harsha is such a king, he's an amazing professor who made stat 203 a very doable and enjoyable class. He's super caring and will make sure everyone understands the concepts. I love how he would emphasize and repeat important concepts in the course. If you do the assignments and keep up with the lectures and course, you can do really well!

Everyone scared me and said STAT 201 is the hardest class in their degree, however Harsha made it one of the easiest! He would make you do all the hard calculations in the assignments that weren't worth much, and the exams which were worth more would have the most basic easiest questions ever. I studied two hours before the final and got full marks

I've had Harsha before and he was fine, but 430 was poorly done for an online class. Basically read the textbook word for word while screen-sharing it. Didn't breakdown the material or add anything helpful. Felt like I was listening to the textbook on Audible. I spent more time memorizing details than learning the reasons behind certain designs.

Harsha was great for STAT 201!! His pre-recorded lectures were straight to the point and he even acknowledged on our first day of class that he would try to be concise as he understands that it's a boring subject for many and did not want them to drag. Exams are easy if you do the homework. R code can be tricky but he didn't put it on the exams.

Professor Perera was a really fair and understanding professor, and adapted very well with the online format. His lectures are very straight forward and include examples, there's about 5-6 assignments (he gives you more than enough time). Do the assignments, watch the lectures, read the textbook, and you should do fine. def take this class w/ him!

Lectures were easy to follow and mostly short. There were a few short quizzes during the semester, and a couple assignments. Assignments could be a little long at times, but similar examples were in the textbook that you could follow along. Exams were multiple choice and open book.

THE BEST PROF DO NOT HESITATE TO TAKE WITH HIM hands down the best prof, he has tons of office hours, he understands peoples situations, adjusts classes to the people that take them unlike some other profs out there. You will not regret taking a class with him, its an easy class in general but having an understanding, well teaching prof is perfect

Harsha is a really really solid prof for STAT 201. He's very organized with all the course material and is one of the most accommodating profs I've had. His lectures are all pre-recorded and he goes through them with a lot of detail (good pacing too). If you put in the work, it's not hard to get an A.
Harsha is a fantastic prof. Gives you the information needed without wasting time. Super clear criteria, easily accessible, very warm personality.

Clear, short lectures that are to the point. Exam quizzes and assignment are all base on thing he gave and assignment basically set you up to ace exams if you put in a few hours every 2 weeks. Extremely understanding and considerate during pandemic and course overall was taught well
Really solid prof for stat 201. Lectures were explained clearly and assignments reflected the exams. Course was not hard if you put time in to study. Perera is a really nice dude.

Honestly by far the most accommodating & considerate prof I've had during the pandemic. Really respectful and kind to students, Harsha genuinely tried his best to accommodate individual needs/difficulties that arose because of COVID. Lecture material is explained clear enough, difficulty is as you'd except of a stats course. Really great guy.

Basically does a bad read-aloud of the textbook for material. Runs the course in a pretty obtuse way where the bulk of your time will be spent working through menial mechanics of experimental design. Seems allergic to technology. Pretty terrible, especially when compared with other profs.
Wouldn't extend an assignment deadline for me and wasn't accommodating during exams the week following the death of two loved family members. Pretty heartless in my opinion.
I handed in an assignment late because I had a family emergency and he told me I get a zero anyways. Idk kind of heartless if you ask me.
The best professor
He was missing for a bunch of classes and didn't give the sub any material to teach.... also he scaled me down so it messed up my mark so bad.... was a big disappointment

"ITs an EaSy GpA bOOstEr" I took stat100 with him thinking it'd be a breeze in the park but oh boy was I wrong. Gamage as a prof is actually great, he listens and can be funny at times. This class had 1 of 2 assessments. Quizzes and exams. His quizzes were not bad but his exams were insanely hard. I'd look elsewhere first if I were you.

Dr. Perera is a really good prof compared to the other statistics profs. His midterms are straight forward (except a few tricky questions) and his lectures are easy to follow. My only complaint about him is that he wasn't in class for like 6 classes out of the semester and his replacement couldn't teach properly - making catching up harder.

A good professor with a fair and consistent difficulty for exams. You know what to expect for the exams and he makes the course concepts relevant and reasonable. His exams are very fair and comprehensive.

Gamage is a caring and attentive prof. He always made sure the concepts were understood. It may have felt boring and repetitive since it was an easy intro class, but it was very helpful towards the end when the material became more complex. It was hard not skipping lectures, but there were top hat quizzes almost every lecture. Overall great prof.

Fun and Easy STATS class, most questions come dierctly from textbook, suggest using the text as it helps for exams and quizzes. Participation in Class matters as tophat quizzes are straightforward and worth 10% of mark. Very fun prof, easy class if you pay attention and do practice questions!

Took Stat 350 with him last semester and I found his teaching abilities above and beyond standards. Pay attention on lectures since not everything useful is found on the slides. He was very flexible if students needed help during and out of office hours. Really liked his attitude towards students which made me respect him very much.

his notes and lectures are very scattered, self taught a lot of material. he spends time on things that are easy and skims over the hard stuff. his marking and homework is fairly easy just sucks because i taught all the hard stuff. also he's is hard to understand due to his accent

Overall I think Perera is a great Teacher, excellent notes and assignments explaining the concepts properly if you take your time to actually study them. Only downside is the need to buy SPSS and learning how to use it can be quite tricky. A majority of the time spent on assignment was probably learning how to use the program. Would recommend!

Good professor overall, the textbook itself is not that clear at explaining concepts but his lecture notes will clarify most of the information needed. Stats 101 requires the use of SPSS which most Stats TA's do not know how to use, so for assignments it would be wise to show to to class and listen to his SPSS demos. Solid class overall

He's a great professor. He takes genuine care into a student's learning; always reassuring, checking, and engaging with the students to make sure we understand. His work load is reasonable, manageable, relevant and super helpful! A nice guy with some humor here and there. Would recommendd

Harha was awesome. He somehow made me look forward to 9am lectures and managed to make the dry content fairly interesting. His notes are straightforward and all of the midterms/finals are based on the assignment questions. Great prof overall, would definitely recommend taking class with him!
I didn't even show up to class ever and got a B, easy ass class with a funny dude.

Good prof but you couldn't understand most of whatever he was talking about during lecture; heavy accent. Class is super easy, textbook is good for supplementary material but super good GPA booster. Everything is straight forward, exams are based off of lecture slides and questions follow the assignment question. Allows cheat sheet for final

Probably one of my best GPA booster course. Content is really easy and interesting. His accent might be a little hard to understand but he clearly explains everything on his lecture slides. It's useful to go to his office hour if you have any question about the assignments. Overall, he is a very caring prof. Highly recommend!
Dude is so chill

Harsha is an amazing prof. He is very specific with his grading criteria. The midterms were very fair and you are told what is expected from you. He allowed a cheat sheet for the final exam, so you don't need to memorize the formulas. He goes through a lot of examples during lecture that help with the 5 assignments and he gives hints for exams.

Really kind and caring stat teacher. Managed very well for a class that a lot of non mathematically inclined students take. He teaches clearly and is always ready to help his students. Great guy, highly recommend.
Harsha is an amazing prof who cares about his students and his course content. He makes an effort to make some of the dry material interesting and is overall a really nice guy.

Harsha is one of the best profs I've ever had. He makes lectures as entertaining as possible cracking jokes now and then. Very passionate about stats and he cares about the students. Despite the difficult class setting, he did an amazing job at teaching. Couldn't recommend him more.

Harsha is by far one of the best profs I've ever had! Midterms and final are straightforward as long as you do practice questions out of the textbook. His teaching style is amazing and he's kind of funny as well. Highly recommend taking Stats 100 with him!!

He an amazing prof! He has the lecture slides available on Canvas before lecture and it has all the key points. The tests are fair and most of the questions are the practice questions from the textbook. He also responds to email very quickly. He's very nice and knows his stuff. I highly recommend taking a class with him!
Kind of boring... I mean stats is not the most exciting field but going to the two hour lecture was really hard!
Easy prof! Take stat100 with him, you won't regret. Just take some time to read his slides than you  will be fine.

His lectures were easy to follow, and there were no surprises on the midterms or final exam. If you take some time to study, you will do well in his class. He's also just a good guy who has an interesting educational background.

To all arts students who are dreading to take stats, PLEASE TAKE IT WITH HARSHA! He makes it very easy to understand concepts, his assignments are rather easy, and the three exams are very fair. The only downside I would say is 5 assignments and 2 midterms in between can seem never ending. I'd also consider taking STAT203 w/ him in the future!
One of the best profs at SFU! His lectures are very easy to follow and he's a funny guy. Just 5 easy assignments, 2 midterms, and 1 final.
He is very helpful and fair. Just read the slides for the midterms and final, and u will be fine!

Even though the concept was dry af, Harsh made it somewhat enjoyable. He is super helpful and approachable during his office hour. His exams are relatively easy. 100% recommend!!! be ready for his cricket love( he shows his cricket video during lecture)

Very helpful during office hours. Goes over everything needed to do well on midterms and final in his lectures just pay attention to his "hints" and examples. Tells you exactly what you need to know and understand to do well in the course. Amazing Prof!

Took my first stats course with Harsha and while intimidated at first, I was pleasantly surprised by the outcome. He is clear about his expectations and truly wants you to learn in his course. He is also very helpful and understanding as others have said as well.  Thank you for a great semester Harsha and keep up the excellent job!

Harsha is a good teacher. he is clear in what he expects and does a good job in explaining concepts in the simplest way possible. He is very helpful and avaliable when needed. He also  listens to students regarding  ( he even accepted a one page cheat sheet on the final)

Great prof! Let's you have a cheat sheet for the exams, which aren't that difficult in the first place. What helped me was taking notes while he goes through ppt slide. Lectures are important because he drops a lot of hints there. As mentioned below, he should probably have more examples in lecture. Be sure to go through the textbook questions!

Harsha was a great prof. No one really wants to take stats and it is usually a requirement. Harsha teaches as if you have never learned any stats ever (which most of us haven't) The best prof to take if you need this requirement of an intro stat. A dreaded and challenging course is taught in great manner! Exam averages are usually 75%

very understanding as he just finished his PH.D recently and knows how it feels to be in our seats. Gives hints for what will be on the exam and a breakdown for the questions by topic. Final exam was more difficult than the midterms but still fair. Get to use cheat sheets for exams. It was my first stats class and he made it easier than I expected

He literally tells you what is on the exam and emphasizes everything you need to know. Really nice guy and a good prof. Sucks that he's leaving to go back to Singapore because he was definitely an assets to the STATS department at SFU!

Harsha provides helpful lecture slides, but you have to take the time to go over them on your own. He didn't provide any example questions which would have been useful when studying for the midterms and the final. Go to lecture and pay close attention, go home and review notes again before exams.

I didnt enjoy this class at all I had to take it. Read the textbook because it explains everything more clearly than he does. He sends out lecture notes so you don't really need to attend class. He is extremely confusing and jumps around on a topic. If you ask a question he just rereads the lecture notes. Tests are fine + assignments, usage of SPSS
